nric_violation.template:
CRITICAL VIOLATION: Unauthorized NRIC Collection

LEGISLATION VIOLATED:
• PDPA Section 18 - Purpose Limitation
• PDPC Advisory Guidelines on NRIC Numbers (2018)

MAX PENALTY: {penalty_amount}

VIOLATION DETAILS:
{details}

EVIDENCE:
{evidence}

DEVELOPER IMPLEMENTATION TASKS:

REQUIREMENTS:
1. Remove NRIC/FIN collection fields from {location} immediately.
2. Ensure no backend logging or database storage of NRIC/FIN numbers unless legally required.
3. Implement alternative identification (e.g., last 4 digits + full name or company-issued ID).
4. Update data collection forms to clearly state the purpose of identity verification.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
• No input fields for NRIC/FIN are present on {location}.
• Database schemas verified to have removed or encrypted NRIC/FIN columns.
• SHA-256 hash of the removal confirmation anchored on {polygon_network_name}.

RECOMMENDED TOOLS:
• Booppa Evidence Anchor ({polygon_network_name})
• PDPC NRIC Guidelines: {nric_guidelines_url}

cookie_violation.template:
HIGH VIOLATION: Missing PDPA-Compliant Cookie Consent Banner

LEGISLATION VIOLATED:
• PDPA Section 13 (Notification Purpose)
• PDPC Advisory Guidelines on Cookies 2021

MAX PENALTY: {penalty_amount} or 10% of annual revenue (whichever is higher)

VIOLATION DETAILS:
Missing PDPA-compliant cookie consent banner. Implied consent is not permitted under Singapore law.

EVIDENCE:
No cookie consent mechanism detected on the website.

DEVELOPER IMPLEMENTATION TASKS:

REQUIREMENTS:
1. Implement a cookie consent banner that appears on first visit before any non-essential cookies are set.
2. Use explicit active opt-in — no pre-ticked checkboxes allowed under PDPA.
3. Provide granular consent categories: e.g. Necessary, Analytics, Marketing, Functional.
4. Support multi-language (at minimum: English and any local language relevant to your user base).
5. Store user consent preferences server-side and respect them on all subsequent visits.
6. Provide a clear 'Manage Preferences' or 'Cookie Settings' link accessible from every page (e.g. in the footer).
7. Ensure no third-party trackers, analytics scripts, or marketing pixels load until consent is granted.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
• Banner visible on first visit with Accept All / Reject Optional buttons.
• No analytics or marketing cookies set before user action.
• User preferences persisted and respected on page reload and return visits.
• Consent log stored with timestamp for audit purposes (required for blockchain anchoring).

RECOMMENDED TOOLS:
• Cookiebot, OneTrust, or Osano
• Booppa Evidence Anchor ({polygon_network_name})

security_violation.template:
CRITICAL VIOLATION: Inadequate Data Protection Measures

LEGISLATION VIOLATED:
• PDPA Section 24 - Protection Obligation
• MAS Notice 644 - Cyber Hygiene

MAX PENALTY: {penalty_amount}

VIOLATION DETAILS:
{details}

EVIDENCE:
{evidence}

DEVELOPER IMPLEMENTATION TASKS:

REQUIREMENTS:
1. DEPLOY HTTPS IMMEDIATELY (Critical for data in transit).
2. Implement security headers: Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS), Content-Security-Policy (CSP), X-Frame-Options.
3. Conduct a full vulnerability assessment of all public-facing endpoints.
4. Document security measures in the Privacy Policy and anchor the version hash on Polygon.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
• All traffic redirected to HTTPS with valid SSL/TLS certificate.
• Security headers verified via Booppa Trust Scan.
• Security implementation certificate anchored on {polygon_network_name}.

RECOMMENDED TOOLS:
• Let's Encrypt (SSL)
• Booppa Trust Intelligence Suite

dnc_violation.template:
MEDIUM VIOLATION: Marketing / DNC Violation

LEGISLATION VIOLATED:
• PDPA Part IX (Do Not Call Provisions)
• Spam Control Act Section 43

MAX PENALTY: {penalty_amount} for DNC violations + S$10,000 per unsolicited message

VIOLATION DETAILS:
No reference to DNC (Do Not Call) Registry compliance in the privacy policy for marketing communications.

EVIDENCE:
DNC Registry not referenced anywhere in the website or privacy policy.

DEVELOPER IMPLEMENTATION TASKS:

REQUIREMENTS:
1. Add a dedicated section to the Privacy Policy stating that the company complies with the Singapore DNC Registry.
2. Clearly explain to users how to opt out of marketing communications (email, SMS, phone calls).
3. Implement a functional opt-out mechanism (e.g. an unsubscribe link in all marketing emails, a 'Do Not Contact Me' form on the website).
4. Ensure all marketing communication workflows check the DNC Registry before sending.
5. Add version control to the Privacy Policy page (e.g. 'Last updated: DD/MM/YYYY, Version X.X').

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
• Privacy Policy page includes explicit DNC Registry compliance statement.
• Opt-out mechanism is functional and tested end-to-end.
• Marketing system queries DNC Registry before dispatch.
• Policy version and update date visible on the page.

RECOMMENDED REFERENCE:
• PDPC DNC Guidelines: https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/guidelines-and-consultation/guidelines/dnc-provisions
• Booppa Privacy Anchor ({polygon_network_name})
